name: Sorrento
intro: Sorrento is where the Peninsula narrows to a point and the social temperature rises. This is old-money beach culture done with increasing sophistication  -  limestone cliffs, a ferry to Queenscliff across the Heads, ocean baths carved from rock at low tide  -  alongside a main street that has quietly evolved into something genuinely worth driving for. The summer crowd is real and relentless, but the town earns it. Off-season Sorrento is a revelation: the light changes, the restaurants empty out to their best selves, and you can walk the ocean beach from one end to the other without encountering anyone but locals. The back beach at dusk is one of the best things on the Peninsula, full stop. Stay at least two nights.

heroImage.alt: Bright morning light on the Sorrento coastline, Victoria

tldr: Sorrento is the Peninsula's most celebrated town — limestone cliffs, ocean baths carved from rock, a ferry to Queenscliff across the Heads, and a main street that has earned its reputation.
Best for: first-time Peninsula visitors; romantic weekends; food and wine visitors who want a tip-of-the-Peninsula base.
Signature experience: ocean baths at dawn, then walk the back beach to the limestone cliffs before the day-trippers arrive.
Two nights minimum to see it properly; off-season is a revelation.
Summer crowds are real — book ahead or come in autumn.
